one_sentence_thesis: Negative feedback enables stable improvement beyond positive-only learning, but far-field reuse can eliminate the finite equilibrium; DRPO preserves useful local repulsion while suppressing the destructive far-field component of the aggregate negative term.
guidance_driven_optimizations: Reclassify Product manifold as historical source-isolation provenance rather than a third primary environment.
Promote quality-distance isolation to the central identification claim and state the exact matched variables.
Separate the already-supported output-score result from the still-needed full-parameter same-ray or Jacobian-gain closure.
Give Theorem 1 one job: explain the Positive-only to stable-extrapolation to boundary/no-finite-equilibrium transition.
Make DRPO modify the same aggregate negative term named by Theorem 1.
Order evidence as external reality anchor, controlled identification, theorem phase validation, and external task closure.
Remove defensive global-convergence and fixed-advantage scope language from the active manuscript story.
Reserve experiment claims for registered status and keep Hopper/Countdown values TBD until formal terminal audits.

gates.evidence: One central useful-to-destructive repulsion tension governs the outline and blueprint.
Every section advances the same causal and methodological arc.
Defensive global-convergence and fixed-advantage scope paragraphs are absent from the active theory story.
Badness-distance isolation is a named Introduction gap, controlled RQ, figure responsibility, and supplementation gate.
Theorem 1 has one job and its detailed proofs are assigned to the appendix.
DRPO maps q m_- to q_lambda m_{-,lambda}, the same term identified by Theorem 1.
Each major claim maps to an environment, RQ, metric, control, and current evidence state.
C-U1 and D-U1 are the primary controlled environments; Product manifold is historical provenance; Hopper and Countdown are external validation.
Task collapse, boundary events, and NaN/Inf numerical failure are kept separate.
Budget matching, paired seeds, best/terminal reporting, and terminal audit remain method-comparison gates.
Related Work claims a missing causal/equilibrium bridge rather than denying prior negative-update research.
The outline preserves current formal statuses and leaves unfinished external results TBD.
Figure 1 and the experiment sequence carry the complete story rather than serving as an inventory.
The six outline and blueprint blocks align in ID, title, order, and parent SHA-256.

minor: C-U1 E1 currently establishes matched-|A| output-score amplification; a same-state/same-ray radial probe or Jacobian-gain decomposition is still required before full-parameter distance-only attribution.
D-U1 common/rare comparisons require a paper-facing audit of badness, semantics, count, and base-coefficient matching.
Hopper and Countdown formal external-anchor results remain unavailable and must stay TBD until terminal audits.
C-U1 E4 method ranking remains gated by deterministic shortlist freeze, exact actor+Adam continuation, TAPER-CONV, and untouched confirmation seeds.

experiment_supplements.item: C-U1 same-state/same-ray radial probe or Jacobian-gain decomposition
Hopper E7 and Countdown formal mechanism diagnostics with terminal audit
E4 shortlist freeze, exact actor+Adam continuation, TAPER-CONV, and confirmation seeds 130-149
